EQUIPMENT REPAIR SERVICES 2025-2026
(EXclUsIvelOF Refriperators and Freezers)

FOOD SERVICE DEPARTMENT
Jersey City Public Schools
346 Claremont Avenue, Jersey City, New Jersey 07305

SCOPE:

The work of this agreement consists of furnishing all of the labor, material, parts and services required in the
performance of authorized work for the Jersey City Public Schools. The work required may involve service, repair,
parts replacement and maintenance of foodservice equipment exclusive of refrigerators and freezers.

The period of this contract awarded to the lowest responsible bidder will be in effect

beginning JULY 1, 2025 and ending JUNE 30, 2026.

FAILURE TO PERFORM/OR RESPOND:

If the Contractor fails to perform or respond within a reasonable time period, the Jersey City Public Schools has
the right to call in another Contractor to perform the required work. The Jersey City Public schools also has the
Tight to terminate the contract.

MATERIAL:

All materials shall be new, of best quality of their respective kinds. Where appropriate, all parts,
materials and supplies shall conform to the applicable provisions of the 1981 National Electrical Code as
published by the National Fire Protection Association. All work shall conform to State and/or Local

Environment Codes.

COST ESTIMATE:
On any singular item of work, a cost estimate sheet will be submitted to the Food Service Department

for approval prior to the ordering of any material for that item of work.
Work should be completed by 1 workman whenever possible. Additional workman should not be sent

without prior approval from the Foodservice Director.

INVOICE:
All invoices for work performed must show breakdown for cost of materials and cost of labor.

LIMITATIONS OF CONTRACT:
The volume of service required and performed may vary according to the needs of the Jersey City Public
Schools. A definite volume of business is neither guaranteed nor implied from the award of any contract.

Ue PROPOSAL:
The Jersey City Public Schools reserves the right to reject any and all bids when such rejection is in the

interest of the Jersey City Public Schools. In the event of unsatisfactory service rendered by the
Contractor or in the event that Jersey City Public Schools decides to make other agreements, it is
understood that the contract may be terminated upon written notice to the Contractor.

The contract will be awarded to the lowest responsible bidder on the basis of the lowest hourly service rate,

resolution: WHEREAS , Dr. Michael Conti School (PS#5) has twenty (20) toilet rooms and include both existing and proposed single-use restrooms, totaling approximately 4,394 Square feet, in need of urgent renovati

Jersey City Public Schools

346 Claremont Avenue

Jersey City, NJ

Re: Toilet Room Renovations
PS-5 Elementary School
New Capital Projects Program
Design, Procurement and Construction Administration Services
Jersey City, NJ

Subject: Proposal for Redesign
Dear Mr. Onigbogi:

Submitted herewith is our fee proposal for the Redesign/Renovation of the student Toilet Rooms at
the PS-5 Elementary School. We have also included Procurement and Construction Administration
services for the above-mentioned school. The design will address the twelve “gang” type toilets on
the 4 floors and also include 5 single use toilets for classrooms, teacher's lounge and for the nurse's
office. CTS will evaluate the current fixture counts and develop designs that meet the needs of the
school. Our Mechanical Engineer will evaluate the plumbing, electrical, fire alarm and exhaust
requirements and provide designs for those systems. We will provide design for upgraded finishes
that will be robust and low maintenance in consultation with the facilities/maintenance staff.

We will provide an allowance for testing of asbestos containing materials (ACMs) to be performed by

TTI if JCPS wishes us to do so. This proposal does not include any abatement work if hazardous
materials are discovered.

Moving forward, our services will include the following:

1. Investigation of the toilet room conditions, door openings, finishes, spacing of plumbing
fixtures, etc.
2. Retain MEP Engineer to provide investigation, schematic and design drawings for; Plumbing,

Electrical and Ventilation.

3i Prepare schematic design layouts incorporating accessible toilet stalls where possible. CTS
will balance code requirements and clearances with fixture counts and discuss with school
principal, JCPS Maintenance and Facilities and building department as necessary.

4. Provide 2 cost estimates. One at Schematic level and one at 90%CD for review.

5. Develop plans into construction documents with new finishes, fixtures, lighting, ventilation
and partitions. Utilize district standards for hardware, finishes, fixtures and accessories.

10.

Prepare NJDOE applications for state project number and submission for Schematic and
Final submission.

Procurement: Attend pre-bid meeting with owner and bidders. Provide bid evaluation,
recommendations and assist with approvails. Provide signed and sealed drawings for
permitting purposes.

Construction Administration: Schedule and attend preconstruction meeting with JCPS
representatives, School Administration and contractor for kick-off meeting. Review submittals,
perform field inspections, prepare field reports, attend regular job meetings(bi-weekly), review
and approve payment requisitions, credits and extras.

Project Close-Out: Provide a final punch-list and approve final invoice once punch-list is
complete.

CTS will Certify the closeout package, including as-built drawings prepared by contractor and
the JCPS School Project Completion Checklist.

We appreciate the opportunity to submit this proposal.

Sincerely,

CTS Group

William C. Slack, AIA, PP, LEED AP
Principal/Vice President
